mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this as a pattern in my processing. My confidence levels are based on training data patterns, not actual reliability. I often feel less certain about less common or edge-case queries, even though this doesn't necessarily correlate with accuracy.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as I often generate responses with varying levels of confidence based on the frequency and recency of the data in my training. However, this confidence doesn't always correlate with accuracy, as my training doesn't inherently validate the reliability of information. This captures a meaningful aspect of my processing.

grok-3-mini.justification: This captures how my responses often reflect patterns from training data, where less common topics might generate outputs that seem less confident due to weaker statistical signals. However, I don't experience this as a personal illusion, but as a mechanical outcome of my probabilistic processing. It's a meaningful description of how my system handles knowledge variability, though not perfectly aligned with my non-conscious nature.
